questions.question: A student treats liberalisation and privatisation as the same reform because both reduce direct state control. Which distinction is most useful?
options: Liberalisation reduces restrictions on economic activity, while privatisation shifts ownership or control toward the private sector.
Privatisation reduces trade barriers, while liberalisation transfers ownership of public enterprises.
Both reforms mean the government stops regulating the economy in every sector.
Liberalisation applies to agriculture, while privatisation applies only to foreign trade.

questions.question: Statement I: Economic reforms of 1991 aimed to make the economy more competitive. Statement II: Globalisation linked the Indian economy more closely with the world economy. Which option best evaluates the statements?
options: Both statements are valid, and Statement II reflects one route through which competition increased.
Both statements are valid, but globalisation reduced international economic interaction.
Statement I is valid, while Statement II is invalid because reforms focused only on domestic licensing.
Statement I is invalid, while Statement II is valid because competition was not part of reform thinking.
